domain: seismic array backazimuth (PDAR), teleseismic P -- GEOPHYSICS: elastic waves, natural earthquake sources
harness: experiments/seismic_flip.py (imports compress3 from rehab033.py -- bit-identical matched-bit three-arm compressor)
array: PDAR (IMS network IM), 13-element short-period array PD01-PD13, aperture ~3.2 km; fetched via IRIS/EarthScope FDSN
consumer: incoherent wideband MUSIC over BACKAZIMUTH at the theoretical teleseismic-P slowness (per-event taup/iasp91 ray parameter), single plane-wave source
ground_truth: the event catalogue BACKAZIMUTH (great-circle from the array to the USGS epicentre). The sealed D3 protocol ('catalogue backazimuth') is HONORED -- NO reference amendment (the 13-element array resolves backazimuth to a few degrees).
data: USGS catalogue M>=6.5 earthquakes, 2017-2018, teleseismic distance 30-95 deg; 34 events cached after the distance filter

config_frozen.note: a GT-free MUSIC-sharpness gate was available but the sweep found it unnecessary (sharp_min=0 = full event set performs best)

split: deterministic CHRONOLOGICAL split: calibration = 17 earlier events (~2017); held-out = 17 later events (~2018) -- DISJOINT events
verdict: CONFIRMED -- all three sealed bars pass against the ABSOLUTE catalogue backazimuth on DISJOINT held-out events. The consumer-relative flip (angle-favoring code beats reconstruction-optimal Lloyd-Max at matched bits on the downstream backazimuth task, while Lloyd reconstructs the raw signal better -- 17/17 recon-trade) replicates on a THIRD distinct physics: seismic elastic waves from natural earthquake sources. Config frozen + sealed (2de7927) before the confirm; no reference amendment (sealed protocol honored).
